PORTFOLIO

Risk Map Flint MI

Concept: Overlapping Risk Factors for Toxic Drinking Water in Flint Michigan. Criteria: -Distance from river (.3 miles) -Distance from TRI facility with greater than 10,000 tons water pollution (.3 miles) -Distance from known lead water service pipes (.2 miles) Skills used: Buffer, Clip, Join, Dissolve, Intersect Findings: Most of Flint is exposed to one or more of three evaluated risk factors for toxic drinking water. Two areas that overlap wards 5, 6, 7, and 8 are exposed to all three. Proposed Solutions: -Request evaluation for Superfund site status to obtain funding for clean up and monitoring. -Replace lead service pipes with PVC. -Add or upgrade water treatment facilities within Flint’s watershed. -Impose stronger regulation on industry for future spills/dumping waste, esp. fines to help pay for service pipe and water treatment upgrades.

2016 Presidential Election PA

Concept: 2016 Presidential election map by county in Pa. Skills used: Joins, add field, calculate field, manipulate symbology Conclusion: Despite the volume of land taken-up by republican voting counties the population of Pa. is concentrated in it's largest cites overall, Philadelphia, Harrisburg, Erie and Pittsburg. Because of the population distribution the state's delegates went to the democrats despite the visual ques indicating the state voted majority republican.

Proximity of Schools to CH4 Producing Landfills

Concept: Proximity of schools to methane producing landfills in LA per supervisorial district. Criteria: -Identify schools within 0.5km of a CH4 producing landfill -Narrow selection to schools within 0.5km of a CH4 producing landfill and within a below mean supervisorial district -Identify schools poorest schools closest CH4 producing landfill Conclusion: District 1 contains 54 of 156 schools in LA found within 0.5km of a methane producing landfill. 37 of the 54 schools within 0.5km of a methane producing landfill are public schools, 16 of the 54 are elementary schools; all the elementary schools are also public. To offer a window of comparison there are 49 schools in District 2 within 0.5km of a methane producing landfill, 14 schools in District 3, 24 in District 4, and 15 in District 5. 65.8% of schools located in a risk zone in district 1 are public elementary schools and studies show that young people are at significant risk from methane exposure.
